Spaceships, building damage, repair bots, and minimum starting AC

- Spaceships fly across canvas, count scales with AC/s rate (max 20)
- Ships can hit buildings: 30% chance/sec within 20px, deals 10 damage
- Buildings have 100 HP, show HP bar when damaged, disabled at 0 HP
- Repair Bot building (200 AC): heals 15 HP/s on direct MST branch
- Repair Bot has 3 upgrade tiers with distinct pixel art
- Every level guarantees at least 50 AC (Collector cost) to start

commit.message:
Batch: Fixes null ErrorMessage when promoting status from MultiStatus response (#5650)

## Summary

Fixes #5649

When a transactional batch response returns **207 MultiStatus** and the
SDK promotes the status code from a failing operation (e.g., 409
Conflict), the \TransactionalBatchResponse.ErrorMessage\ was always \
ull\. This is because only \StatusCode\ and \SubStatusCode\ were
promoted from the failing operation — the \ErrorMessage\ remained
sourced from the outer 207 response, which has no error message.

## Root Cause

In \TransactionalBatchResponse.PopulateFromContentAsync()\, the status
promotion logic (lines 365–379) correctly promotes \
esponseStatusCode\ and \
esponseSubStatusCode\ from the first failing operation result, but the
\ErrorMessage\ passed to the \TransactionalBatchResponse\ constructor
was still \
esponseMessage.ErrorMessage\ — the outer 207 MultiStatus message's
error, which is always `null`.

\\\csharp
// Before (bug):
TransactionalBatchResponse response = new TransactionalBatchResponse(
    responseStatusCode,              // 409 - promoted from operation ✅
    responseSubStatusCode,           // promoted from operation ✅
    responseMessage.ErrorMessage,    // null from outer 207 response ❌
    ...);
\\\

## Fix

When promoting the status code from a failing operation, also read the
error message from the failing operation's \ResourceStream\ (which
contains the server's error body for that specific operation) and use it
as the promoted \ErrorMessage\.

\\\csharp
// After (fix):
if (result.ResourceStream != null)
{
    using (StreamReader reader = new StreamReader(
        result.ResourceStream, encoding: Encoding.UTF8,
        detectEncodingFromByteOrderMarks: true,
        bufferSize: 1024, leaveOpen: true))
    {
        responseErrorMessage = reader.ReadToEnd();
result.ResourceStream.Position = 0; // Reset so stream is still usable
    }
}
\\\

The stream position is reset after reading so the \ResourceStream\
remains available to consumers who access individual operation results.

## Testing

Added \BatchResponseDeserializationPromotesErrorMessageAsync\ test that:
1. Creates a 409 Conflict operation result with a \ResourceStream\
containing an error body
2. Creates a second operation result with 424 FailedDependency (as the
server would)
3. Wraps them in a 207 MultiStatus response
4. Calls \FromResponseMessageAsync\ with
\shouldPromoteOperationStatus=true\
5. Asserts \StatusCode\ is promoted to 409
6. **Asserts \ErrorMessage\ is promoted from the operation's
ResourceStream** (not null)

All 103 existing batch-related unit tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
content: add 10 Crime and Punishment articles from different angles

Based on Dostoevsky's original Russian text (Преступление и наказание).
Each article features Russian quotes with translations and analysis:

1. The Opening - first paragraph analysis
2. Marmeladov's Confession - poverty vs destitution
3. The Horse Dream - the most disturbing chapter
4. The Extraordinary Man Theory - Raskolnikov's philosophy
5. Porfiry Petrovich - the psychological detective
6. Sonya Marmeladova - the Lazarus scene
7. St. Petersburg as Character - the sick city
8. Svidrigailov's Ghosts - the dark mirror
9. The Epilogue Problem - does the ending work?
10. Вместо диалектики наступила жизнь - the key sentence

Dates spread from Jul 2025 to Feb 2026.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add --once mode to autoSnip for terminal one-shot snip

Running 'open -W autoSnip.app --args --once' from terminal:
- Captures display 1 silently (screencapture -x -D 1)
- Sends via AirDrop to configured device
- Exits when done (no menu bar, no persistent process)

Also adds snip.app bundle (Sources/snip.swift) — not yet fully working,
--once mode via autoSnip.app is the preferred approach as it reuses the
already-trusted bundle identity and Accessibility permissions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add TilePreview component (#20)

Canvas-based tile renderer with nearest-neighbor scaling for pixel art
workflows. Accepts ImageData, HTMLCanvasElement, ImageBitmap, URL string,
or null sources.

Features:
- Checkerboard/black/white/none background modes for transparency
- Optional label overlay (bottom-center, semi-transparent)
- Pixel grid overlay at high zoom levels (4x+)
- Border variants: none, default, selected, error
- Click/hover handlers with keyboard activation
- Handles null/empty source with placeholder
- HiDPI-aware canvas rendering

Includes tests, Storybook stories with mock tile helper.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(dashboard): remove SSE response compression and harden push error handling

- Remove text/event-stream from compression mime types — compressing
  long-lived SSE streams causes ArgumentOutOfRangeException in
  StreamPipeWriter.FlushAsyncInternal when clients disconnect
- Fix heartbeat timer: use task{} with proper async error handling
  instead of fire-and-forget ContinueWith that left unobserved exceptions
- Wrap semaphore release in try/finally to prevent deadlock on exception
- Add ArgumentOutOfRangeException + InvalidOperationException to Datastar
  SSE handler catch filters (was only in JSON handler)
- Downgrade transport error logging from error to debug level
- Add OperationCanceledException to JSON SSE push catch filters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove activity titles from output to avoid biasing model

Activity names (e.g. 'Easy Run', 'Hard Workout') can bias the model's
interpretation. Now only showing objective data:
- Date/time
- Activity type (Run, Ride, etc.)
- Performance metrics
- Activity ID

Updated tests to check for types instead of names.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement screen transition system with demos and enhancements (#5)

* feat: add Screen Transition API design and implementation plan documentation

* feat: update transition demo phase with detailed tasks and acceptance criteria

* feat: Add offscreen rendering and transition management

- Implement offscreen render pass in VulkanContext for enhanced rendering capabilities.
- Introduce Transition and TransitionManager classes for managing screen transitions.
- Add unit tests for OffscreenRenderTarget and TransitionManager to ensure functionality and stability.
- Create various transition types (Fade, Wipe, CircleReveal) with corresponding shader paths and uniform updates.
- Enhance TransitionManager to handle multiple transitions, including instant transitions and cancellation.
- Ensure RAII safety and proper resource management in OffscreenRenderTarget.

* Implement screen transition system and demo

- Completed all phases of the screen transition implementation plan.
- Added TransitionManager to handle transitions between scenes.
- Introduced various transition effects (fade, wipe, circle reveal) in the new transition demo.
- Updated Game class to support scene transitions with visual effects.
- Enhanced VulkanContext to allow custom command buffer recording for transitions.
- Created smoke tests for the transition demo to ensure functionality.
- Updated documentation and examples to reflect the new transition features.

* feat: Add built-in scene transitions (Fade, Wipe, CircleReveal)

* feat: Add BlockFall transition implementation and tests

* feat: Enhance BlockFall transition with border detection and drift effect

* feat: Implement shader file tracking and synchronization for tools and examples

* feat: Sync audio demo assets and remove imgui_demo example

* feat: Add pause, frame-step, and playback speed controls for transitions

* feat: Add support for freezing source scene during transitions

* feat: Enhance sorting of executable entries by incorporating git commit recency

* feat: Display relative executable paths in debug UI for better context

* feat: Add benchmark scripts for capturing and comparing compile timings

* feat: Add guides for creating and verifying skills in the VDE project

* Initial plan

* docs: fix TransitionUniforms and TransitionDirection in API.md

Co-authored-by: derekdk <3893574+derekdk@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Update API-DOC.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: fix OffscreenRenderTarget::create signature - add missing VkRenderPass parameter

Co-authored-by: derekdk <3893574+derekdk@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Move vkDeviceWaitIdle from OffscreenRenderTarget::destroy() to callers

Co-authored-by: derekdk <3893574+derekdk@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add null handle check before vkDeviceWaitIdle calls in TransitionManager

commit.message:
fix: properly sign app bundle for Sparkle update validation

- Add codesign step in release workflow to apply proper ad-hoc
  bundle signature (sealing Info.plist and Resources) after export.
  The linker-only signature from xcodebuild with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO
  left Info.plist unbound and Resources unsealed, causing Sparkle to
  reject all updates as improperly signed.
- Fix appcast.xml 1.3.0 entry with re-signed zip signature and size.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Restructure otel-plugin configuration with layered overrides (#21896)

* Restructure otel-plugin configuration with layered overrides

Split plugin_config.rs into a module with submodules (endpoint, metrics,
logs, env) for maintainability.

Configuration is now resolved in three layers, each overriding the previous:
1. Stock config (otel.yaml shipped with Netdata)
2. User config (partial YAML overrides, unknown fields ignored)
3. Environment variables (NETDATA_OTEL_* prefix, highest priority)

Each layer is logged as a single JSON line (stock/user/effective) for
easy debugging via journalctl.

* Update src/crates/netdata-otel/otel-plugin/src/plugin_config/mod.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle explicit null in custom YAML deserializers for override config

* Propagate user config errors instead of silently falling back to stock

* Validate TLS CA cert requires cert/key and reject non-UTF-8 env vars

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Ctrl+C and Escape work during retry/streaming

Root cause: _send_message was called with 'await' from the event
handler, blocking the entire Textual event loop. Key bindings
(Ctrl+C, Escape) couldn't fire until the method returned.

Fix:
- Run _send_message as a background worker via run_worker()
  so the event handler returns immediately
- Escape (action_cancel) sets is_streaming=False, which breaks
  the retry countdown loop, the streaming loop, and resets the
  status bar to Ready
- Ctrl+C (action_quit) also sets is_streaming=False before exit
- Streaming loop checks is_streaming on each chunk for fast cancel
- Guard against double-sends at the top of _send_message

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Security hardening for public internet exposure

- Add helmet with strict CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options
- Add rate limiting: 200 req/min global API, 10 login attempts per 15 min
- Remove CORS wildcard (same-origin only)
- Persist JWT secret in database (survives restarts)
- Protect settings routes with admin auth when already configured
- Add input validation middleware (username format, field lengths, LDAP injection)
- Strengthen LDAP escape to cover all RFC 4515 special chars + control chars
- Run Docker container as non-root 'admars' user
- Reduce JSON body limit from 10mb to 1mb

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

cleanup(kernels): remove 15 orphaned source files (~18.6K lines) (#3346)

Supersedes #3343 (recreated for CI trigger).

Remove 15 source files in bitnet-kernels that exist on disk but are not
declared in any mod.rs or lib.rs module tree. These files are dead code
unreachable by the compiler and untestable.

| Category | Files | Lines |
|----------|-------|-------|
| NEON | neon_attention.rs, neon_gemv.rs | ~3K |
| OpenCL | 12 files (autotuner through telemetry) | ~14K |
| GPU | gpu/benchmarks.rs | ~1.5K |
| **Total** | **15 files** | **18,591 lines** |

Combined with #3340 (50 NEON files, 76K lines): **~95K lines of dead
code removed**.

### Receipt
- **What changed**: 15 files deleted, 1 file doc comment improved
(lib.rs)
- **What ran locally**: \cargo build --no-default-features --features
cpu -p bitnet-kernels\ builds clean
- **CI relied on**: Build & Test, Clippy
- **Parity evidence**: N/A (deletion only)
- **Docs touched**: bitnet-kernels lib.rs doc comment improved
- **Risks**: none  files are verifiably not in module tree
- **Disposition**: MERGE (A)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7511)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* Enforce lint/refactor boundary: remove planFeatherRenames from lint workspace

target-state.md §2.3, §2.4, §3.4 require that rename-conflict planning — a
cross-file, project-aware operation — live exclusively in @gml-modules/refactor.
The refactor workspace already owns a full implementation via
RefactorEngine.planFeatherRenames and ProjectAnalysisProvider.planFeatherRenames.

The lint workspace violated this boundary by:
- Exposing planFeatherRenames on GmlProjectContext and ProjectAnalysisSnapshot
- Advertising RENAME_CONFLICT_PLANNING as a ProjectCapability
- Implementing rename-conflict logic in project-analysis-provider.ts
  (which had a TODO comment acknowledging this exact violation)

No lint rule ever called planFeatherRenames; the duplication was dead weight.

Changes:
- src/lint/src/types/index.ts: remove RENAME_CONFLICT_PLANNING from ProjectCapability
- src/lint/src/services/index.ts: remove GmlFeatherRenamePlanEntry + planFeatherRenames from GmlProjectContext
- src/lint/src/services/project-analysis-provider.ts: drop TODO, GmlFeatherRenamePlanEntry import,
  RENAME_CONFLICT_PLANNING capability, and planFeatherRenames from both snapshot impls
- src/lint/src/services/project-lint-context-registry.ts: remove planFeatherRenames proxy
- src/lint/test/rules/rule-fixtures.test.ts: remove RENAME_CONFLICT_PLANNING + planFeatherRenames mock
- src/lint/test/rules/project-context-registry.test.ts: same
- src/lint/test/services/project-context-boundary.test.ts: new boundary-enforcement tests (3 passing)

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update validator requirements and onboarding process for Polkadot (#1513)

* docs: update validator requirements and onboarding process for Polkadot

- Added sections on minimum validator self-stake of 10,000 DOT and minimum commission of 10% effective from March 2026 runtime upgrade in requirements.md.
- Updated onboarding guide to reflect the new unbonding period changes expected in April 2026 and the minimum commission requirement.
- Introduced a new document for Staking Operator Proxy, detailing its setup and operational guidelines for non-custodial validator management.
- Clarified slashing penalties and the new Dynamic Allocation Pool for slashed funds in offenses-and-slashes.md.
- Updated rewards documentation to include the minimum commission enforcement and its implications for validators.

* docs: standardize terminology in Staking Operator Proxy documentation

- Removed backticks from "Staking Operator" for consistency throughout the document.
- Updated sections to ensure uniformity in the naming of the Staking Operator proxy.
- Enhanced clarity in the setup and operational guidelines for non-custodial validator management.

* docs: enhance Staking Operator Proxy documentation for clarity and formatting

- Improved table formatting for better readability.

commit.message:
Add -Registration switch to Generate-FlowBody.ps1

Two modes:
- -Registration: outputs fixed 3-field body for /api/register-form
  (no prompts, instant output)
- Default: interactive, asks for question count + titles, outputs
  body for /api/process-response

commit.message:
Fix cmake.buildTask build failures not aborting debug launches (#4785)

* Initial plan

* Fix build diagnostics and exit code not propagating when using cmake.buildTask (#4489)

When cmake.buildTask is true, the CustomBuildTaskTerminal captures build
output but never forwards it to the CMakeBuildConsumer for diagnostic
parsing. This means compiler errors never appear in the Problems tab.

Additionally, setBuildProcessForTask always resolved with exit code 0
regardless of the actual build result, causing failed builds to appear
successful (related to #3389).

Changes:
- Forward build output from CustomBuildTaskTerminal to an external
  OutputConsumer so diagnostics are parsed during task builds
- Pass the consumer through resolveInternalTask to the terminal
- Use onDidEndTaskProcess instead of onDidEndTask to capture the
  actual exit code from the build task

Co-authored-by: Omotola <8052624+Omotola@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert #4489 changes, keep only #3389 fix (build task exit code propagation)

Co-authored-by: Omotola <8052624+Omotola@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stale target path on build failure by using direct exit code promise from CustomBuildTaskTerminal (#3389)

Co-authored-by: Omotola <8052624+Omotola@users.noreply.github.com>

* Dispose onDidClose listener after resolving exit code

Co-authored-by: Omotola <8052624+Omotola@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge main into copilot/fix-build-task-error-reporting, resolve CHANGELOG conflict

Co-authored-by: Omotola <8052624+Omotola@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix brace-style lint error in cmakeTaskProvider.ts

Co-authored-by: Omotola <8052624+Omotola@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

repository.name: vscode-cmake-tools
repository.description: CMake Tools provides a robust, convenient workflow for CMake projects in VS Code. It simplifies configurations with CMake presets, supports IntelliSense and built-in debugging for CMake scripts, and offers configurable tasks. Its customizable interface allows for efficient project control, while also simplifying test execution and monitoring.

commit.message:
Add GitHub Repo Doctor custom agent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: HTTP-level request/response tracing for Azure OpenAI

Adds httpx event hooks to the Azure OpenAI client to log:
- Full request body: message count, per-message sizes, tool defs,
  all params (model, stream, stream_options, etc.)
- Response rate limit headers: x-ratelimit-remaining-requests,
  x-ratelimit-remaining-tokens (reveals actual TPM/RPM usage)
- Error response bodies for 4xx/5xx (full Azure error message)

This traces what's actually on the wire vs what our app-level
logging shows, to diagnose the TPM limit discrepancy.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
